pullRequest.title: Add comprehensive GitHub Copilot development instructions with complete documentation integration
pullRequest.body:
This PR adds comprehensive GitHub Copilot instructions that combine validated development workflows with detailed architecture knowledge from the complete codebase documentation. The instructions provide step-by-step guidance for working effectively with the Solace Agent Mesh distributed AI system.

## Enhanced Content

**Comprehensive Architecture Coverage**: The instructions now include detailed information about all core subsystems:
- **Agent Framework** (`agent/`): Complete ADK integration, tool development, and A2A protocol support
- **Common Infrastructure** (`common/`): Foundational A2A communication, type systems, and client/server implementations  
- **Core A2A Service** (`core_a2a/`): Reusable service layer for agent interactions
- **Gateway Framework** (`gateway/`): HTTP/SSE, Slack, and Webhook integrations for external platforms
- **Orchestrator Agent**: Centralized workflow management and task coordination capabilities
- **Solace PubSub+ Event Broker**: Complete A2A protocol topic structure and message routing patterns

**Complete Development Patterns**: Includes practical code examples and usage patterns for:
- Creating and configuring ADK agents with YAML and programmatic setup
- Building custom tools with artifact management and error handling
- Implementing inter-agent communication via PeerAgentTool
- Developing gateways with FastAPI dependencies and SSE streaming
- Working with multimedia processing tools (audio, image generation)
- Client-side A2A integration with streaming responses

**Built-in Tool Groups**: Comprehensive documentation of tool configuration including:
- Artifact management tools (create, load, append, extract content)
- Data analysis tools (SQL queries, SQLite, JQ transforms, Plotly charts)
- Web tools (HTTP requests, scraping)
- Audio tools (text-to-speech, multi-speaker)
- Communication tools (peer agent delegation)

**Project Structure & Configuration**: Complete guidance on:
- Standard project organization and file discovery rules
- Shared configuration management with YAML anchors and environment variables
- Plugin ecosystem and deployment patterns
- Docker deployment with custom builds

**Debugging & Troubleshooting**: Comprehensive debugging approaches including:
- Component isolation techniques
- VSCode debug configuration
- STIM file analysis for stimulus lifecycle tracing
- Broker monitoring and direct agent testing methods

**Tutorial Workflows**: Common integration scenarios covering:
- SQL database integration with natural language queries
- RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) with vector databases
- MongoDB document operations
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server integration
- Custom agent creation and Slack/Event Mesh gateway setup

**Validated Build Commands**: All commands tested with measured timing expectations and explicit "NEVER CANCEL" warnings for long-running operations like environment setup (10-15+ minutes) and stress tests (can take hours).

**Domain-Specific Knowledge**: The instructions incorporate deep architectural knowledge from both `src/llm_detail.txt` and the complete `docs/docs/documentation` directory, enabling GitHub Copilot to understand the complex distributed agent system beyond basic setup and testing workflows.

The enhanced instructions save developers time by providing both practical development commands and the comprehensive domain expertise needed to work effectively with the sophisticated agent mesh architecture across all documented features and use cases.

Fixes #264.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💬 Share your feedback on Copilot coding agent for the chance to win a $200 gift card! Click [here](https://survey3.medallia.com/?EAHeSx-AP01bZqG0Ld9QLQ) to start the survey.

pullRequest.title: fix(DATAGO-124721): Fix dash in agent-name breaking agent configuration
pullRequest.body:
### What is the purpose of this change?

    This PR fixes an issue where agent names containing dashes were causing problems with agent configuration, as reported in GitHub issue #276. The solution implements agent name sanitization to ensure only alphanumeric characters and underscores are used.

### How was this change implemented?

    High-level approach:
    - Added agent name validation in `SamAgentApp` to detect invalid characters
    - Implemented sanitization logic to convert dashes and other special characters to underscores
    - Added comprehensive unit tests to verify the sanitization works correctly for various cases
    - Added warning logs to notify when agent names are modified

    Key commits:
    - feat: add agent name sanitization tests for various cases
    - GH#276 - Fix dash in agent-name breaking agent configuration

### Key Design Decisions

    We chose to automatically sanitize agent names rather than throwing errors to maintain backward compatibility and provide a better user experience. This approach allows users to continue using their existing agent names while ensuring they work properly with the system.

### How was this change tested?

- [x] Manual testing: Verified agents with dashes in names work correctly after sanitization
- [x] Unit tests: Added extensive test cases for various agent name formats including dashes, special characters, spaces, and mixed valid/invalid characters
- [ ] Integration tests: Not applicable
- [ ] Known limitations: None identified

### Is there anything the reviewers should focus on/be aware of?

    Review the sanitization logic to ensure it handles all edge cases appropriately and that the warning messages are clear.


Closes #276

pullRequest.title: feat(DATAGO-124720): Enhance registry management with re-add functionality and cache clearing
pullRequest.body:
### What is the purpose of this change?

This change enhances the registry management functionality by allowing users to re-add (update) existing registries instead of rejecting them as duplicates. It also improves data freshness by implementing git cache clearing when registries are re-added, ensuring the latest plugins are always fetched.

### How was this change implemented?

High-level approach:
- Modified `add_registry` method to return a tuple `(success, is_update)` indicating whether the operation was an add or update
- Added functionality to update existing registries instead of rejecting as duplicates
- Implemented `clear_git_cache` method to remove cached git repositories
- Enhanced plugin scraping to support force fresh cloning when registries are re-added
- Updated API endpoints and frontend to handle and properly display registry updates
- Added comprehensive unit tests for all new functionality

Key commits:
- feat: Enhance registry management with re-add functionality and cache clearing

### Key Design Decisions

I chose to implement registry re-adding by modifying the existing `add_registry` function to return both success status and whether it was an update operation, rather than creating a separate update endpoint. This keeps the API simpler and allows the frontend to handle both cases with a single flow.

For cache clearing, I implemented a targeted approach where only the affected registry's cache is cleared on re-add, rather than clearing all caches. This provides a better balance between data freshness and performance.

### How was this change tested?

- [x] Manual testing: Adding a registry, modifying it externally, re-adding it with the same URL and verifying it updates with fresh data
- [x] Unit tests: Added comprehensive test suite in `test_registry_readd.py` covering registry re-adding, cache clearing, and API integration
- [x] Integration tests: Verified the API endpoint properly handles re-adding and returns the correct is_update flag
- [ ] Known limitations: The cache clearing could be more targeted in some cases - currently it may clear more data than strictly necessary

### Is there anything the reviewers should focus on/be aware of?

The change to the return value of `add_registry` may affect any external callers of this function who expect a boolean return.

Closes #277

pullRequest.title: feat(DATAGO-114896) Add OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials support for LLM provider authentication
pullRequest.body:
- Implement OAuth2TokenManager for secure token management with automatic refresh
- Add support for configurable token endpoints, client credentials, and scopes
- Support custom CA certificates for token endpoint verification
- Update configuration templates and environment files with OAuth settings
- Add comprehensive documentation for OAuth2 authentication
- Include unit and integration tests for OAuth functionality

Fixes #390

pullRequest.title: chore(DATAGO-124718): Update LLM provider model lists to current versions
pullRequest.body:
For Matt

---

## Summary
- Updates model dropdown options in the configuration portal to reflect currently available models
- Fixes issue #664 (Outdated Gemini Models in Configuration Frontend Dropdown)

## Changes
- **OpenAI**: Add GPT-5 family, GPT-4.1 variants, o3, o4-mini; remove deprecated models
- **Anthropic**: Add Claude 4.5 (Sonnet, Opus, Haiku) and Claude 4 models
- **Google Gemini**: Add Gemini 3 preview and Gemini 2.5 stable models; remove deprecated 1.5/2.0 experimental models
- **AWS Bedrock**: Add Nova Premier, Nova 2 models, and Claude 4.x variants

## Test plan
- [x] Run `sam init` and verify updated models appear in dropdowns for each provider
- [x] Confirmed Gemini models against current Google AI /models endpoint
- [x] Confirmed OpenAI models against current OpenAI /models endpoint
- [x] Confirmed Anthropic models against current Anthropic /models endpoint

Closes #664

pullRequest.title: fix(DATAGO-130051): update tool name and description handling for DynamicTool subclasses, closes #1255
pullRequest.body:
### What is the purpose of this change?

This PR fixes how DynamicTool subclasses are handled when generating tool manifests and scope maps. Previously, these tools could show up with placeholder values ("dynamic_tool_placeholder") in the agent card tool manifest instead of their real names and descriptions.

### How was this change implemented?

High-level approach involves checking for tool_name/tool_description properties on DynamicTool subclasses before falling back to name/description attributes:

- fix: update tool name and description handling for DynamicTool subclasses
- Updated tool name retrieval in both the scopes map builder and manifest builder
- Added unit tests to verify both DynamicTool and regular tool handling

### Key Design Decisions

The implementation uses property accessors (tool_name, tool_description) to get the real values from DynamicTools rather than relying on the inherited name/description attributes which often contain placeholders until the tool is actually invoked.

### How was this change tested?

- [x] Unit tests: Added two comprehensive test files covering both the scopes map builder and the manifest builder
- [x] Tests verify that DynamicTools are properly identified by their real names, not placeholders
- [x] Tests validate mixed scenarios with both DynamicTool and regular tool types
- [ ] Known limitations: This change does not impact runtime tool declaration - only affects agent card manifest and scope maps

### Is there anything the reviewers should focus on/be aware of?

Pay attention to the property accessor pattern used to retrieve real names from DynamicTool subclasses and verify this is compatible with all DynamicTool implementations.

Closes #1255
